skill.name: youtrack
skill.description: Use YouTrack CLI workflows for IdeaVim tickets, comments, tags, status updates, and release version management.
skill.summary: Manage IdeaVim YouTrack tickets with documented CLI commands.
skill.author: JetBrains

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are true in the sense that the skill documents many npx tsx commands, but they are not Ruby backtick execution. The commands are legitimate YouTrack workflows, yet they can mutate tickets through a token-backed external service, so publication should include a warning. The hardcoded URL is the expected JetBrains YouTrack host, and the weak-cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 appears to be a false positive.

medium_findings.title: Token-backed external issue tracker mutations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run npx tsx YouTrack CLI commands that add comments, manage tags, set status, and create or delete release versions. These actions require YOUTRACK_TOKEN and can change external YouTrack data, so users should review commands before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly documents token use and multiple mutation commands. The intent appears operational rather than malicious, which limits severity.

low_findings.title: Static analyzer misclassified Markdown command examples
low_findings.description: The analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution across many lines, but the evidence is Markdown inline code and fenced bash examples. The examples still represent external commands users may run, but there is no executable Ruby backtick code in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows Markdown code formatting, not Ruby execution syntax. The commands are documentation for local CLI usage.

low_findings.title: Expected hardcoded YouTrack service URL
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to the JetBrains YouTrack project used by the skill. This is an expected endpoint for an issue-tracker integration and does not indicate data exfiltration by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL matches the stated YouTrack integration purpose. No evidence found of hidden endpoints or unauthorized network destinations.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography finding appears false positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md line 3, but that line is descriptive front matter for the skill. No evidence found of cryptographic code, hashing, encryption, or credential transformation in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains only the skill description. The reviewed file does not contain crypto APIs or algorithm names used as code.

dangerous_patterns.title: External command execution guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides many npx tsx commands for local execution. These commands should be treated as user-approved actions because they can contact YouTrack and modify project state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and repeated. Risk depends on user execution and token scope, so this is operational risk rather than confirmed abuse.

content.value_statement: IdeaVim contributors need consistent YouTrack workflows instead of GitHub Issues. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code the right ticket commands, tags, and release actions.
actual_capabilities: Identifies VIM-prefixed YouTrack tickets and the correct JetBrains project URL.
Provides commands for adding public or private ticket comments.
Documents commands for adding and removing known YouTrack tags.
Shows how to set ticket status and fix version fields.
Supports release version creation and deletion workflows.
Explains common IdeaVim workflows such as analyzed, clarification, and ready-to-release states.
limitations: Requires a valid YOUTRACK_TOKEN before CLI commands can work.
Depends on repository scripts outside this single skill file.
Does not validate ticket IDs, tag IDs, or permissions by itself.
Can change live YouTrack data if commands are executed.

use_cases.title: Triage IdeaVim Tickets
use_cases.description: Review a VIM ticket, add the analyzed tag, and request clarification when needed.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Private Review
use_cases.description: Add a private YouTrack comment for JetBrains team review without using GitHub Issues.

use_cases.title: Prepare Release Updates
use_cases.description: Set fix versions, update ticket status, and manage release versions during release preparation.

prompt_templates.title: Get Ticket Details
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the YouTrack skill to show me how to fetch details for VIM-1234.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Private Comment
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare the YouTrack command to add a private clarification comment to VIM-1234 for JetBrains review.

prompt_templates.title: Mark Analysis Complete
prompt_templates.prompt: For VIM-1234, provide the YouTrack workflow to mark the ticket as analyzed and explain what will change.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Release Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a safe step-by-step YouTrack workflow for setting VIM-1234 to Ready To Release and assigning fix version 2.29.0.

output_examples.input: How do I ask for clarification on VIM-1234?
output_examples.output: Use the pending clarification tag, then add a private comment that clearly states the question for the JetBrains team.

output_examples.input: I need to prepare VIM-1234 for release 2.29.0.
output_examples.output: The response lists the fix-version command, the ready-to-release status command, and a reminder to confirm the token and ticket ID first.

output_examples.input: Can I use GitHub Issues for VIM-1234?
output_examples.output: The response explains that IdeaVim uses YouTrack for VIM tickets and points to the YouTrack workflow instead.

best_practices: Confirm the ticket ID and intended YouTrack field change before running any command.
Use private comments only for information that should remain visible to the JetBrains team.
Keep YOUTRACK_TOKEN scoped and avoid sharing command output that may expose private issue data.
anti_patterns: Do not use GitHub Issues for VIM tickets in this project.
Do not run mutation commands without reviewing the exact ticket, tag, status, or version.
Do not paste secrets or unrelated personal data into YouTrack comments.

faq.question: What project does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports IdeaVim tickets in JetBrains YouTrack, using ticket IDs such as VIM-1234.

faq.question: Does it use GitHub Issues?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly directs users to YouTrack for this project.

faq.question: What credential is required?
faq.answer: The documented CLI scripts require the YOUTRACK_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: Can it add private comments?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents the private comment flag for comments visible only to the JetBrains team.

faq.question: Can it manage release versions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes commands to create, delete, and assign release versions.

faq.question: Is it safe to run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. Review each command first because the commands can modify live YouTrack issues.
